activities_projects.title: Character Sketch – Lomov
steps: Read the play carefully
List 5 traits of Lomov with examples from the text
Write a short paragraph describing his character
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will analyze character traits and textual evidence.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of traits and examples, clarity of paragraph

activities_projects.title: Character Sketch – Natalia
steps: Identify 5 traits of Natalia with supporting lines from the play
Write a paragraph describing her character
Discuss how her traits create humor in the play
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop understanding of characterization and comic elements.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification, textual support, and clarity

activities_projects.title: Dialogue Act-Out
activities_projects.objective: Practice speaking skills and understand play dynamics.
steps: Choose a humorous scene from the play
Assign roles and act out the dialogue
Focus on expression, tone, and timing
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop oral expression and understanding of humor in plays.

activities_projects.title: Sequence of Events Chart
activities_projects.objective: Understand the sequence of the play’s plot.
steps: List main events of the play in order
Draw a simple chart showing cause-effect relationships
Share findings with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop organizational and analytical skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of sequence and clarity of presentation

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Building
activities_projects.objective: Learn and use new words from the play.
steps: Identify 10 new words from the play
Write meanings and use each in a sentence
Create a short paragraph using 5–6 words
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will improve vocabulary and contextual usage.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct meanings, sentence construction, and paragraph clarity

activities_projects.title: Comic Strip Creation
activities_projects.objective: Visualize humorous moments of the play.
steps: Select one funny scene
Create a 4–5 panel comic strip showing the action and dialogues
Label emotions and actions clearly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand humor and sequencing through visual representation.

activities_projects.title: Short Speech – Humor in Relationships
activities_projects.objective: Discuss humor and misunderstandings in relationships.
steps: Prepare a 1–2 minute speech on humorous misunderstandings in relationships
Use examples from the play
Present confidently in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop speaking skills and relate literature to life.

activities_projects.title: Comparison Chart – Characters’ Reactions
activities_projects.objective: Compare how Lomov and Natalia react in conflicts.
steps: List 3 conflicts in the play
Write reactions of Lomov and Natalia for each
Draw a comparison chart and discuss in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will analyze character behavior and interpersonal dynamics.

activities_projects.title: Alternate Ending Activity
steps: Imagine an alternate ending for the play
Write a short scene showing a different resolution
Include dialogue and character reactions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop creativity and understanding of plot structure.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, logical flow, and textual consistency

activities_projects.title: Role Reflection Journal
activities_projects.objective: Reflect on characters’ emotions and motives.
steps: Write a journal entry as either Lomov or Natalia
Describe your thoughts and feelings during key moments
Reflect on why certain reactions occurred
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will deepen empathy and analytical understanding of characters.
activities_projects.evaluation: Emotional insight, textual relevance, and clarity
